rationale: Both candidates miss nearly all the ground-truth reviewers' core findings (the High-severity stream-slot-leak, the cumulative/delta rewind silent bug, the ceil-division latent bug, and most of the non-blocking docstring/cleanup/generalizability comments); Y gets partial credit for brushing against the legacy-checkpoint/v2-tokenizer issue via its load_weights note. Y's precision is hurt more seriously, though: it explicitly asserts the slot-leak lifecycle 'looks complete' and that vendored files 'carry a proper... attribution header', both of which directly contradict the ground truth (a fixed High-severity bug and an explicit reviewer ask for a missing provenance header) — confident wrong claims are worse than omissions. X's five flagged findings (preprocess type-guard regression, hardcoded sampling params, missing tests) are grounded and actionable but don't overlap with GT and it never engages the codec/legacy-checkpoint files where the real bugs live. Y is more thorough and its actionable suggestions are slightly better specified, but the false-confidence issues tip this narrowly to X.
